questions.question: State one key advantage and one key disadvantage of a shared-memory system compared to a distributed-memory system. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Advantage: Easier to program due to a global address space and implicit data sharing.
Advantage: Often allows for dynamic load balancing.
Disadvantage: Limited scalability due to memory/bus bandwidth constraints.
Disadvantage: Requires complex cache coherence protocols, adding overhead.

questions.question: Compare and contrast shared-memory and distributed-memory architectures. Your answer should cover memory organization, programming model, scalability, and a key hardware challenge for each. (8 marks)
expectedPoints: Memory Organization: Shared-memory has a single global address space; Distributed-memory has private per-processor memory.
Programming Model: Shared-memory uses multithreading (e.g., OpenMP); Distributed-memory uses message passing (e.g., MPI).
Scalability: Shared-memory is limited to 10s-100s of cores; Distributed-memory is highly scalable to 1000s+ of cores.
Hardware Challenge: Shared-memory must manage cache coherence; Distributed-memory relies on a high-speed, low-latency interconnect network.
Data Sharing: Implicit in shared-memory via memory reads/writes; Explicit in distributed-memory via send/receive operations.
Cost: Shared-memory systems are more expensive to scale; Distributed-memory uses commodity hardware for cost-effective scaling.
